setting.note: One man's lifelong search for a house of his own, across colonial Trinidad. The map runs from the sugar country of Caroni where Mohun Biswas is born, through the market town of Chaguanas and the family compound he marries into, to the capital, Port of Spain, and the small house in St James that is finally his.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. V. S. Naipaul, first published 1961. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: A House for Mr Biswas, V. S. Naipaul (1961). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of Trinidad's cane country, where the story begins and Biswas spends his life trying to leave. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
